project_type: Python cognitive agent prototype - Virtual SIA with concept formation, theory runtime, anomaly detection
build_system: setuptools via pyproject.toml, no external dependencies
test_framework: pytest, tests in tests/ directory, test classes organized by module
verification_instructions: Run pytest, confirm 88+ existing tests pass AND new tests pass
setup_instructions: No setup needed - pure Python, no external deps
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network, Python 3.12.13 via pyenv, pure Python project with no external dependencies
contribution_requirements: All documentation in Arabic. 'Legitimate Theft' methodology MANDATORY for all new mechanisms - each must document source using Arabic template with sections: ما الذي أخذناه؟ / ما الذي لم نأخذه الآن؟ / ماذا أصبح عندنا؟. New thefts start at 5.33. New features gated behind parameters (default OFF). Existing 88 tests must continue passing.
key_patterns: 1) Anomaly leverage pattern: functions like verify_output_anomaly_aware, choose_tier_anomaly_aware with docstrings containing Arabic theft docs. 2) Pipeline gating: use_anomaly_leverage: bool = False parameter pattern. 3) Theory runtime: LocalTheoryObject with scope, predictive_claims, mechanism_claims. 4) Registry pattern: InMemoryTheoryRegistry with add_theory/list_theories. 5) DataObject pattern: BaseObject with @classmethod create(), slots=True dataclass.
directory_structure: virtual_sia/core/objects/ - data models (theory.py, anomaly.py, task.py, etc). virtual_sia/runtime/ - runtime modules (theory_runtime/, verification_runtime/, concept_engine/, economy_control/, pipeline/). tests/ - pytest tests. Root - markdown docs in Arabic.
